reference_spec_or_target: 400V-class, 60 kW continuous minimum, 90 kW peak reference
sourcing_strategy: Use Bosch SMG180 OHW as reference spec only; Bosch direct is Tier 1/B2B, not an individual-builder purchase path
key_questions: Which SMG180 variant, inverter, gearbox, CAD, and control docs correspond to an obtainable Fiat 500e salvage unit?
notes: Matches current motor reference in docs/02-powertrain-notes.md, but sourcing must not assume Bosch consumer sales.

reference_spec_or_target: Used/salvaged Fiat 500e Bosch SMG-family motor, preferably with inverter and harness
sourcing_strategy: Search eBay, salvage yards, and EV dismantlers for complete Fiat 500e motor/inverter assemblies
candidate_source: eBay Fiat 500e Bosch SMG180 salvage search
key_questions: Can a complete Fiat 500e motor/inverter/harness set be sourced with matching part numbers, resolver interface, coolant fittings, HV connectors, and a known controller path?
notes: This is the only currently tracked individual-builder path; marketplace listings remain unverified until exact part numbers and condition are documented.

reference_spec_or_target: 300-430V compatible, compact, documented CAD
sourcing_strategy: Use as high-power alternate if SMG180 sourcing stalls
key_questions: What is delivered price, lead time, voltage configuration, gearbox strategy, and CAN/control documentation?
notes: Likely overpowered for the first reference truck but well documented.

reference_spec_or_target: Motor, inverter, VCU, radiator, pump, HV/LV harness bundle
sourcing_strategy: Ask Hypercraft for a de-rated 400V package compatible with 35-55 kWh pack
key_questions: Can Hypercraft quote a low-power commercial package without locking battery choice?
notes: Good integration path, but may be expensive and less open.

reference_spec_or_target: Final wheel torque suitable for local utility use; no ICE transmission dependency
sourcing_strategy: For Bosch path, prefer the matched Fiat 500e reduction/final-drive hardware if available; otherwise require documented ratio and shaft/flange interface
key_questions: What Fiat 500e reduction/final-drive parts are needed, and can they be sourced with the motor/inverter package?
notes: Critical before chassis motor placement is frozen.

reference_spec_or_target: Kei-truck-scale axle, serviceable bearings, parking-brake compatibility
sourcing_strategy: Use donor kei rear axle for prototype or design custom IRS/live axle around common parts
key_questions: Which donor axle width, hub pattern, and payload rating best fits the reference track width?
notes: The Acty diagrams expose rear axle, driveshaft, brakes, and parking brake categories.

reference_spec_or_target: Serviceable joints, boots, and replacement parts
sourcing_strategy: Use donor kei geometry first; avoid one-off shafts until suspension is frozen
key_questions: Which donor model offers repeatable CV and boot sourcing?
notes: Include boots, clamps, seals, axle nuts, and spares in later BOM.

reference_spec_or_target: Dual-channel pedal preferred; controller-compatible output
sourcing_strategy: Use EV/industrial pedal with known controller compatibility
key_questions: Which pedal output is required by selected VCU/inverter: dual analog, CAN, PWM, or switch?
notes: Treat pedal plausibility and limp behavior as safety requirements.

reference_spec_or_target: 400V-class PDU, fused outputs for inverter, charger, DCDC, heater
sourcing_strategy: Use commercial PDU where possible; custom only after schematic review
key_questions: Can a configurable low-volume PDU be purchased with ratings, CAD, and service documentation?
notes: Existing safety architecture already requires contactors, fuse, precharge, HVIL, and isolation monitoring.

reference_spec_or_target: Pack voltage/current rated, auxiliary contacts preferred
sourcing_strategy: If not integrated in pack/PDU, source sealed EV contactors through EV suppliers
key_questions: What coil voltage, economizer, aux contacts, and interrupt rating are required?
notes: Main positive, main negative, and charge contactors may differ.

reference_spec_or_target: Resistor and contactor/relay sized for inverter DC-link capacitance
sourcing_strategy: Use PDU-integrated precharge or documented EV supplier kit
key_questions: What precharge target time and final voltage delta does the inverter require?
notes: Do not hand-wave this; it affects contactor life and startup faults.

reference_spec_or_target: DC-rated, fault-current compatible, serviceable
sourcing_strategy: Use EV-rated fuses matched to pack fault current and wire size
key_questions: What interrupt rating is required for the final pack and PDU layout?
notes: Fuse choice depends on pack fault-current data from the battery supplier.

reference_spec_or_target: Accessible, touch-safe, HVIL-capable if possible
sourcing_strategy: Prefer pack-integrated MSD; otherwise source EV disconnect hardware
key_questions: Does the selected pack include an MSD and where is it accessible in a kei bed/cab layout?
notes: Must be documented in service procedure.

reference_spec_or_target: All service covers/connectors interrupt enable path
sourcing_strategy: Use pack/PDU/inverter supported HVIL when available
candidate_source: Open Kei Truck HV safety architecture
key_questions: Which components expose HVIL pins and what is the fail-safe state?
notes: Internal repo reference; validate with supplier pinouts.

reference_spec_or_target: 400V-class IMD or pack/PDU-integrated equivalent
sourcing_strategy: Prefer integrated pack/PDU IMD; otherwise source standalone automotive IMD
key_questions: Is isolation monitoring included, and what CAN/fault behavior is exposed?
notes: Required for safe HV operation and diagnostics.

reference_spec_or_target: Orange jacket, voltage/current/temperature rated, abrasion protected
sourcing_strategy: Use EV conversion suppliers for small quantities; industrial cable only with proper rating
key_questions: What cable gauge is required for 60 kW continuous and 120 kW peak at lowest pack voltage?
notes: Coordinate with PDU, inverter, charger, and battery terminals.

reference_spec_or_target: Touch-safe, keyed, sealed, serviceable
sourcing_strategy: Prefer connectors used by selected components; avoid mixing connector families unnecessarily
key_questions: Which connector family is available for battery, inverter, charger, and PDU?
notes: Track crimp tooling as part of the BOM.

reference_spec_or_target: J1772 first prototype target; NACS only after integration path is clear
sourcing_strategy: Use EV conversion inlet with pilot/proximity documentation
key_questions: Which onboard charger and EVCC expect J1772 pilot/proximity wiring?
notes: Keep charge port placement clear of bed cargo abuse.

reference_spec_or_target: 120/240VAC Level 1/2, pack-voltage compatible, CAN or enable input
sourcing_strategy: Use charger compatible with selected pack/BMS and charge inlet
key_questions: What max charge voltage is allowed by selected battery and can it be limited below 430V?
notes: Battery decision drives charger more than body design.

reference_spec_or_target: 300-430V input, 13.8-14.4V output, >=1.5-2 kW target
sourcing_strategy: Use EV-rated converter with sealed connectors and enable/fault pins
key_questions: What 12V load budget is needed for lights, fan, wipers, radio, pumps, and controls?
notes: Panasonic notes the DCDC is the bridge between HV battery and LV loads.

reference_spec_or_target: 400V pack support, CAN, contactor control, thermal inputs
sourcing_strategy: Use pack-integrated BMS when possible; Orion only for custom pack path
key_questions: If custom pack is used, how many cells, taps, temperatures, and CAN messages are needed?
notes: Current battery strategy prefers integrated BMS.

reference_spec_or_target: Coordinates inverter, BMS, charger, DCDC, dash, fault states
sourcing_strategy: Use supplier-provided VCU if buying turnkey powertrain; otherwise define open VCU later
key_questions: Can selected motor/inverter operate with documented open CAN or supplied VCU?
notes: Do not pick VCU before inverter protocol is known.

reference_spec_or_target: SOC, speed, warnings, HV faults, coolant temp, drive state
sourcing_strategy: Use CAN-ready EV gauges/dash compatible with Orion/AEM/Thunderstruck or custom CAN bridge
key_questions: Which CAN data source is authoritative for SOC, speed, and critical warnings?
notes: A simple cluster is better than a tablet-only prototype.

reference_spec_or_target: Bidirectional pack current and voltage for dash/logging
sourcing_strategy: Use shunt or pack/BMS telemetry; avoid duplicate sensors unless needed
key_questions: Does selected BMS/PDU already expose calibrated pack current and voltage?
notes: Needed for range and fault logging.

reference_spec_or_target: Rejects motor/inverter heat at low road speed
sourcing_strategy: Use compact automotive/UTV radiator or supplier kit
key_questions: What heat rejection is required at continuous hill/load case?
notes: Hypercraft bundles radiator and pump in some systems; copy that completeness.

reference_spec_or_target: Brushless 12V pumps sized to motor, inverter, battery loops
sourcing_strategy: Prefer supplier-matched pump; otherwise source EV/motorsport pump
key_questions: What flow and pressure drop do motor, inverter, and battery require?
notes: Bosch reference motor requires water-glycol flow per datasheet.

reference_spec_or_target: Serviceable fill/bleed points and abrasion-safe routing
sourcing_strategy: Use motorsport/industrial hose and clamps rated for coolant and temperature
candidate_source: McMaster-Carr pipe, tubing, hose, and fittings
key_questions: Where are high points and service bleed points in cab-over packaging?
notes: Small packaging errors here become miserable service work.

reference_spec_or_target: Windshield defrost and cabin comfort without ICE coolant
sourcing_strategy: Use EV PTC heater only after HV PDU capacity is reserved
key_questions: What heat output is required for defrost in target climates, and is HV or 12V better?
notes: Defrost is a functional requirement if road use is pursued.

reference_spec_or_target: Small AGM or LiFePO4 buffer battery with safe charge profile
sourcing_strategy: Use common powersports/automotive battery sized by LV load and DCDC behavior
key_questions: What reserve time is needed for hazards, unlock, and shutdown sequence?
notes: LV battery chemistry affects DCDC setpoint.

reference_spec_or_target: Serviceable fused branches and labeled relays
sourcing_strategy: Use sealed automotive fuse/relay module; document every circuit
key_questions: How many fused circuits are needed for lighting, wipers, pumps, VCU, dash, radio, and fans?
notes: Keep low-voltage service separate from HV service.

reference_spec_or_target: Automotive wire, sealed connectors where exposed, labeled service loops
sourcing_strategy: Build prototype harness from documented connector families; mature to loom drawings
key_questions: Which connectors are common to lights, dash, VCU, sensors, and body harness?
notes: Harness documentation should become a repo artifact later.

reference_spec_or_target: Exploded diagrams for body, glass, seats, belts, lights, steering, brakes
sourcing_strategy: Use Honda Acty HA4 diagrams as the first whole-truck checklist baseline
key_questions: Which donor generation best matches the target cab/bed proportions?
notes: This is the strongest found example of a category-complete kei truck parts list.

reference_spec_or_target: Frame/model-specific part numbers and diagrams
sourcing_strategy: Use Amayama, JP-CarParts, and EPC-data for cross-checking part names and assemblies
key_questions: Which frame code should become the reference donor for geometry?
notes: Good for part number discovery; availability must still be verified.

reference_spec_or_target: Honda frame-code-specific parts lookup
sourcing_strategy: Use to cross-check HA3/HA4/HA6/HA7 parts and diagrams
key_questions: Which Acty frame has best fit to target cab, payload, and parts availability?
notes: Useful companion to Honda-Acty.com diagrams.

reference_spec_or_target: Simple sheet/tube cab structure; serviceable removable panels
sourcing_strategy: Fabricate reference cab from sheet/tube; use donor diagrams for panel list
key_questions: Which panels are structural versus bolt-on service panels?
notes: Do not assume donor crashworthiness transfers to custom structure.

reference_spec_or_target: Latchable weather-sealed cab doors with serviceable glass
sourcing_strategy: Use donor kei door hardware for first prototype or industrial hinges/latches for mule
key_questions: Can donor door latches, handles, regulators, and seals be sourced repeatably?
notes: Door system includes hinges, check straps, handles, lock cylinders, striker plates, seals, and drains.

reference_spec_or_target: Exterior/interior handles, locks, striker plates
sourcing_strategy: Use kei donor parts if using kei door geometry
key_questions: Which handles are available new versus used, and can lock cylinders be keyed alike?
notes: Include emergency interior release behavior in design.

reference_spec_or_target: DOT-marked laminated automotive glass if road use is pursued
sourcing_strategy: Use existing kei windshield for prototype geometry or commission custom laminated glass
candidate_source: Auto Glass Japan Honda Acty windshields
key_questions: Which windshield model gives best size/curvature and is available in the U.S. or export channel?
notes: Honda Acty examples are listed around the target kei scale.

reference_spec_or_target: DOT-marked laminated automotive glass if road use is pursued
sourcing_strategy: Use Carry glass if Suzuki cab proportions become reference
candidate_source: Auto Glass Japan Suzuki Carry windshields
key_questions: Does Carry glass better match desired cab width and rake?
notes: Glass often drives cab tooling decisions.

reference_spec_or_target: Prototype/custom glass with automotive safety documentation
sourcing_strategy: Commission only after cab opening is frozen
key_questions: What tooling, minimum order, DOT mark, and lead time apply for custom windshield glass?
notes: Custom glass is high leverage but can become expensive quickly.

reference_spec_or_target: Complex laminated or tempered prototype glass
sourcing_strategy: Use for concept/prototype if OEM kei glass does not fit
key_questions: Can they produce small-run DOT-marked automotive glazing for a new vehicle?
notes: Reserve for later design phase.

reference_spec_or_target: Tempered side/rear glass or approved equivalent
sourcing_strategy: Use minitruck replacement glass where geometry fits; custom later
key_questions: Which door/rear glass sizes are stocked and what markings/certifications are present?
notes: Include regulators, channels, run seals, and weatherstrips.

reference_spec_or_target: Water management, low wind noise, serviceable seals
sourcing_strategy: Use donor seals for donor geometry; industrial push-on seals for flat panels
key_questions: Which seals are automotive-weather suitable and compatible with paint and glass thickness?
notes: Design doors around available seals, not the other way around.

reference_spec_or_target: Kei-scale flat bed, corrosion protected, tie-down capable
sourcing_strategy: Fabricate from steel/aluminum sheet and formed ribs; use donor dimensions as reference
key_questions: Steel or aluminum for v0.1, and what gauge balances payload, cost, and corrosion?
notes: Metal bed should be bolt-on/removable for pack and rear drivetrain service.

reference_spec_or_target: Fold-down side gates with latch and removable/repairable skins
sourcing_strategy: Fabricate flat panels; use Suzuki/Honda latch patterns as inspiration
candidate_source: Honda Acty rear floor and gate diagrams
key_questions: What bed side height and latch position are ergonomic for loading?
notes: Replicate kei utility behavior rather than pickup-truck styling.

reference_spec_or_target: Fold-down tailgate with chains/cables and serviceable latch
sourcing_strategy: Fabricate panel; source latches/hinges from mini-truck or truck-body suppliers
key_questions: Which tailgate latch/hinge family is available new and has acceptable load rating?
notes: Mini4x4 lists Suzuki side/tailgate handles and bed hinge pieces.

reference_spec_or_target: Drop-side and tailgate hinges, corrosion resistant
sourcing_strategy: Use OEM-style kei hinges if geometry matches; otherwise truck/trailer hinges
key_questions: What hinge pin diameter, material, and service access are needed?
notes: Avoid hidden hinges that trap dirt or cannot be replaced.

reference_spec_or_target: Positive latch, easy glove operation, no accidental release
sourcing_strategy: Use kei replacement latches first; industrial tailgate latches for custom panels
key_questions: Are latch bolt spacing and load ratings documented?
notes: Suzuki Carry side/tailgate handles are a useful first pattern.

reference_spec_or_target: Limit tailgate opening and support light cargo while open
sourcing_strategy: Use OEM kei chains if available or truck body cable supports
candidate_source: Buyers Products truck and trailer hardware
key_questions: What open-tailgate load case should be supported?
notes: Include protective sleeves to avoid paint wear.

reference_spec_or_target: Cargo restraint points; bed accessory compatibility
sourcing_strategy: Use truck-body hardware and weld/bolt-on pockets
candidate_source: Buyers Products truck and trailer hardware
key_questions: What tie-down load rating and spacing are required?
notes: Bed accessories should be modular and replaceable.

reference_spec_or_target: Protect high-wear bed rail and gate contact points
sourcing_strategy: Use universal kei gate protectors or cut sheet rubber
key_questions: Do protectors fit custom bed geometry or should we design a generic trim section?
notes: Good low-cost wear item for work use.

reference_spec_or_target: Standard rectangular tube, sheet, tab-and-slot brackets
sourcing_strategy: Use common stock sizes available from multiple suppliers
key_questions: Which minimum material catalog should the chassis standardize on?
notes: Repo scope already prefers standard tube and sheet.

reference_spec_or_target: DXF/STEP-driven repeatable plates and brackets
sourcing_strategy: Use SendCutSend or similar for prototype tab-and-slot parts
key_questions: What tolerances and bend radii are needed for self-fixturing brackets?
notes: Good fit for open-source repeatability.

reference_spec_or_target: Repeatable chassis/bed tube cuts and slots
sourcing_strategy: Use Xometry or local tube laser once CAD is stable
key_questions: Is cost acceptable versus simple saw/notch fabrication for community builds?
notes: Use only where it improves repeatability enough to justify cost.

reference_spec_or_target: Chassis/tube design, cut lists, bend data
sourcing_strategy: Use only if tube chassis gets complex; CAD should remain exportable
key_questions: Does the project need Bend-Tech-specific files or can STEP/DXF cover contributors?
notes: Avoid locking open-source users into a paid tool.

reference_spec_or_target: FMVSS/DOT compliant if road use; durable 12V lamps
sourcing_strategy: Use DOT sealed/LED units or OEM kei lamps only after compliance check
candidate_source: Buyers Products DOT truck and trailer lights
key_questions: What lamp size/beam pattern fits the cab and target regulatory path?
notes: Lighting placement needs FMVSS review before final body holes.

reference_spec_or_target: Amber, visible, hazard compatible
sourcing_strategy: Use OEM kei assemblies or DOT universal lamps
key_questions: Which lamp assemblies are available left/right and what connector do they use?
notes: Mini4x4 lists Suzuki front turn signal kits and rear lamp assemblies.

reference_spec_or_target: FMVSS path dependent, red/amber as required
sourcing_strategy: Use DOT truck/trailer lamps and reflectors
candidate_source: Super Bright LEDs commercial truck/trailer lights
key_questions: What exact reflector/lamp count is required for LSV versus full truck path?
notes: Do not rely on styling lamps for compliance.

reference_spec_or_target: Red stop/tail/turn, sealed, 12V
sourcing_strategy: Use DOT universal truck lamps or kei replacement lamps
key_questions: Round/oval/kei-OEM packaging, and what connector/pigtail should be standardized?
notes: Truck-Lite example lists FMVSS/CMVSS/DOT/SAE compliance family.

reference_spec_or_target: White reverse signal, sealed, 12V
sourcing_strategy: Use DOT/commercial truck lamps
candidate_source: Buyers Products DOT truck and trailer lights
key_questions: Is reverse lamp required for target classification and what switch source triggers it?
notes: VCU gear state can drive reverse lamps.

reference_spec_or_target: White plate illumination
sourcing_strategy: Use DOT trailer/truck license lamp
candidate_source: Buyers Products DOT truck and trailer lights
key_questions: Where does plate mount without conflicting with tailgate?
notes: Include service access to bulb/connector.

reference_spec_or_target: LED-compatible, 12V, visible telltale
sourcing_strategy: Use automotive switch/relay compatible with selected lamps
key_questions: Will the system use a conventional flasher, body controller, or VCU output?
notes: LED lamps may require compatible relay or electronic body control.

reference_spec_or_target: 12V road horn, fused relay
sourcing_strategy: Use universal automotive horn or donor kei horn
key_questions: Where is horn protected from water but acoustically effective?
notes: Acty diagrams include horn and relay category.

reference_spec_or_target: Two-speed or intermittent preferred; clears selected windshield
sourcing_strategy: Use donor kei wiper system if windshield geometry matches
key_questions: Which motor/linkage works with chosen windshield width and cowl packaging?
notes: Yokohama lists Suzuki wiper linkage and motor examples.

reference_spec_or_target: 12V washer pump and serviceable reservoir
sourcing_strategy: Use donor kei or universal compact washer kit
key_questions: Where can reservoir be filled without tilting cab or removing cargo?
notes: Defrost/visibility path includes washers.

reference_spec_or_target: Road-use visibility; foldaway preferred
sourcing_strategy: Use kei replacement mirrors or universal commercial mirrors
key_questions: Which mirror stalks fit the door/A-pillar design and provide rear visibility?
notes: Minitrucks.net lists Suzuki and Daihatsu mirror examples.

reference_spec_or_target: Compact, durable, low H-point, serviceable upholstery
sourcing_strategy: Use compact off-road/commercial seats or donor kei seats after mounting review
key_questions: What seat width, slider travel, head restraint, and mounting strength are required?
notes: Do not assume aftermarket off-road seats satisfy road-use requirements.

reference_spec_or_target: FMVSS-oriented commercial seating path if road certification is pursued
sourcing_strategy: Ask commercial seat supplier about narrow cab-compatible seats
key_questions: Can any seat fit kei-width packaging and provide compliance documentation?
notes: Likely more expensive but stronger evidence path.

reference_spec_or_target: FMVSS 209 belts; anchorages designed/tested separately
sourcing_strategy: Use FMVSS-labeled belts and design anchors to applicable requirements
key_questions: Which belt length/retractor orientation fits the cab and where are upper anchors located?
notes: Seat belt assembly compliance is not the same as vehicle anchorage compliance.

reference_spec_or_target: FMVSS 209 for belt assemblies
sourcing_strategy: Use eCFR as requirement source
key_questions: Which additional standards apply to anchorages, seats, and head restraints for chosen vehicle path?
notes: Track as compliance resource, not a supplier.

reference_spec_or_target: Compact wheel, horn integration, ergonomic reach
sourcing_strategy: Use donor kei wheel/column or aftermarket wheel with compatible boss
candidate_source: Honda Acty steering wheel search baseline
key_questions: Which hub spline/column is selected and how is horn/airbag absence handled?
notes: Marketplace listings are unverified but show donor availability.

reference_spec_or_target: Collapsible strategy, U-joints, firewall bearing, serviceable mounts
sourcing_strategy: Use donor kei column for mule; engineer final collapsibility after regulatory path is clear
key_questions: Does target path require collapsible column testing or documentation?
notes: Steering column is safety-critical; avoid casual parts-bin swaps.

reference_spec_or_target: Manual rack first; geometry-matched width and travel
sourcing_strategy: Use kei replacement rack if suspension geometry follows donor dimensions
key_questions: Which rack length, travel, ratio, and inner pivot spacing match final suspension?
notes: Mini4x4 lists Suzuki racks, tie rods, boots, and drag rods.

reference_spec_or_target: Custom rack dimensions if donor rack geometry fails
sourcing_strategy: Use motorsport supplier only after geometry is calculated
key_questions: What rack length, pinion angle, ratio, and load rating are needed?
notes: Good technical path but less accessible than common donor parts.

reference_spec_or_target: Simple serviceable dash, switch labels, telltales, fuse access
sourcing_strategy: Use Acty dash categories as checklist; fabricate simple panel
key_questions: Which controls/telltales are mandatory for chosen regulatory path?
notes: Include drive selector, charge indicator, hazard, defrost, wiper, lights, and HV fault.

reference_spec_or_target: 12V Bluetooth/FM, compact, optional weather-resistant
sourcing_strategy: Use simple single-DIN head unit; marine unit if cab is work/washdown oriented
key_questions: Is radio required in base BOM or optional accessory?
notes: User asked for radio; keep it low-priority and modular.

reference_spec_or_target: Small sealed speakers and hidden/roof antenna
sourcing_strategy: Use marine/UTV audio parts or donor kei speaker openings
key_questions: Where do speakers fit without stealing knee room or door water paths?
notes: Radio should not complicate the electrical architecture.

item: Floor mat, insulation, headliner, sun visors
reference_spec_or_target: Durable work-truck trim, removable floor
sourcing_strategy: Use donor patterns for checklist; fabricate simple washable trim
key_questions: What trim is required for noise, heat, and occupant contact safety?
notes: Keep prototype trim simple but do not ignore sharp-edge protection.

reference_spec_or_target: Manual or boosted hydraulic system sized for vehicle mass
sourcing_strategy: Use donor kei pedal/master for mule; re-size after final mass and tire radius
key_questions: What pedal ratio, bore size, and front/rear bias suit EV mass distribution?
notes: Regenerative braking does not replace service brakes.

reference_spec_or_target: Disc brakes preferred, service parts available
sourcing_strategy: Use donor kei or compact car/UTV hubs/brakes after load review
candidate_source: Gold Star Parts mini truck parts
key_questions: Which rotor/caliper/pad family is available and rated for target mass?
notes: Gold Star specializes in mini-truck brakes and related parts.

reference_spec_or_target: Drum or disc, parking brake integration
sourcing_strategy: Use donor rear axle brakes if using donor axle
key_questions: Does selected rear axle provide a simple parking brake path?
notes: Parking brake compatibility may decide rear brake strategy.

reference_spec_or_target: DOT-compatible hard lines/flex lines, service loops
sourcing_strategy: Use standard automotive brake plumbing and documented flare/fitting sizes
key_questions: What flare standard and fitting family should the project standardize?
notes: Document routing away from HV and abrasion zones.

reference_spec_or_target: Mechanical hold on grade; serviceable cable path
sourcing_strategy: Use donor rear brake cables if axle is donor; custom cables later
key_questions: What grade-hold requirement and cable routing apply?
notes: Include adjustment access.

reference_spec_or_target: Simple geometry, serviceable bushings and ball joints
sourcing_strategy: Use donor kei front suspension for first rolling mule or fabricate around common service parts
key_questions: Which donor has easiest parts availability and compatible track width?
notes: Suspension choice drives rack, brakes, wheels, and cab floor packaging.

reference_spec_or_target: Payload-capable, simple mounts, predictable handling
sourcing_strategy: Use donor live axle springs/dampers or fabricate leaf/coil mounts
candidate_source: Gold Star Parts mini truck parts
key_questions: Target payload and rear pack placement determine spring rate.
notes: Do not pick springs before battery/bed mass is estimated.

reference_spec_or_target: Replaceable wear items with part numbers
sourcing_strategy: Favor donor components stocked by kei suppliers
key_questions: Which wear parts are available new in the U.S. or by reliable import?
notes: A clever suspension is a bad open-source choice if wear parts are obscure.

reference_spec_or_target: Common bolt pattern, load-rated, brake clearance
sourcing_strategy: Use donor bolt pattern initially; final wheels must meet load and regulatory path
key_questions: What bolt pattern, offset, center bore, load rating, and tire clearance are required?
notes: Track spare tire storage early.

reference_spec_or_target: DOT road tires if road use; payload/load index documented
sourcing_strategy: Use common small truck/passenger tire sizes rather than obscure ATV-only sizes
candidate_source: Gold Star Parts mini truck parts
key_questions: Which tire size balances load, gearing, brake clearance, and replacement availability?
notes: Avoid off-road-only tire assumptions for a public-road path.

reference_spec_or_target: If LSV path is chosen: speed and equipment requirements from FMVSS 500
sourcing_strategy: Use eCFR as authoritative baseline and state-by-state registration later
key_questions: Is the first public-road target LSV, off-road utility, kit, or full truck?
notes: FMVSS 500 has required lamps, mirrors, reflectors, windshield, belts, VIN, and more.

reference_spec_or_target: FMVSS 108 reference for road-use lighting
sourcing_strategy: Use DOT/SAE lamps and confirm placement requirements
key_questions: Which requirements apply for selected vehicle classification?
notes: This should drive lamp placement, not styling alone.

reference_spec_or_target: DOT-marked glazing and supplier documentation
sourcing_strategy: Require markings/documentation from glass source
key_questions: What glazing markings and certification evidence are needed for the chosen path?
notes: Handbook notes DOT markings and manufacturer codes for glazing.

reference_spec_or_target: Part 565 VIN and manufacturer portal workflow
sourcing_strategy: Track only if project becomes a manufacturer rather than prototype design
key_questions: Who would be the manufacturer of record for any road vehicle?
notes: Legal path is explicitly not frozen in docs/01-vehicle-requirements.md.

reference_spec_or_target: Replacement parts, diagrams, model-specific categories
sourcing_strategy: Use for Acty donor research and exact assembly names
key_questions: Can they supply body, glass, steering, lighting, seat, and belt items in quantity or only maintenance parts?
notes: Strong Acty-specific resource.

reference_spec_or_target: DA/DB Carry parts, manuals, electrical diagrams
sourcing_strategy: Use for Suzuki donor research and replacement part pricing
key_questions: Can they quote body/bed hardware and manuals for a reference donor generation?
notes: Yokohama also lists DA62T/DA63T parts.

reference_spec_or_target: Parts across Suzuki, Daihatsu, Honda, Subaru, Mitsubishi
sourcing_strategy: Use as cross-model availability check
key_questions: Which models have the broadest new OEM/aftermarket body and mechanical part support?
notes: Claims long-running JDM mini-truck parts export experience.

reference_spec_or_target: Body, glass, accessories, upgrades, mirrors
sourcing_strategy: Use for U.S. shipping and practical replacement-part availability
key_questions: Which products are OEM replacement versus accessory/off-road only?
notes: Good source for body/glass and accessory categories.

reference_spec_or_target: Suzuki lighting, steering, body and bed hardware
sourcing_strategy: Use for visible pricing and fitment notes
key_questions: Can they provide bulk quote and fitment drawings for bed/lamp/steering components?
notes: Useful for blinkers, tail lamps, latches, hinges, and steering pieces.

reference_spec_or_target: Brakes, lift kits, bumpers, filters, belts, suspension
sourcing_strategy: Use for wear part availability cross-check
key_questions: Which brake/suspension parts are highway appropriate versus off-road only?
notes: Watch supplier off-road disclaimers.

reference_spec_or_target: Fasteners, hinges, latches, seals, tubing, fittings
sourcing_strategy: Use for repeatable non-automotive standard hardware
key_questions: Which parts have adequate material, load, corrosion, and temperature ratings?
notes: Excellent for prototypes, but not a substitute for automotive validation.

reference_spec_or_target: Hinges, latches, stake hardware, DOT lamps
sourcing_strategy: Use for bed hardware and lighting standards
key_questions: Can product drawings/CAD and load ratings be attached to BOM items?
notes: Good fit for work-truck bed hardware.

reference_spec_or_target: 80 kW/254 Nm for 2013-2017 AZE0; later ZE1 units support 110 kW or 160 kW depending inverter/year
sourcing_strategy: Use as primary affordable OEM salvage candidate with Thunderstruck or Resolve-EV control path
key_questions: Which Leaf generation gives best cost, control support, voltage compatibility, and rear/IRS packaging for the kei truck?
notes: Strongest current practical alternative to Bosch because motor, inverter, reduction drive, differential, and VCU support can be sourced as a system.

reference_spec_or_target: 150 kW/360 Nm, permanent-magnet drive unit, ~350V-class donor vehicle
sourcing_strategy: Use as high-power salvage alternate only after controller path is proven
candidate_source: GM Authority Chevrolet Bolt EV specs
key_questions: Is there a repeatable controller/inverter path, and can the drive unit be packaged without overpowering the reference truck?
notes: Attractive power-per-dollar, but currently less boring/repeatable than Leaf for open-source conversion.

reference_spec_or_target: 144V-class SRIPM kit; ~38-39 kW 60-min, ~90-95 kW 2-min peak
sourcing_strategy: Use as lower-voltage DIY alternate if the project creates a reduced-power mule branch
key_questions: Would a 90-180V architecture be accepted for a prototype, or does it conflict too much with the 300-430V battery target?
notes: Good DIY ecosystem and regen support, but lower continuous power and lower voltage than the current reference architecture.

reference_spec_or_target: 72-144V AC kit family; AC-50 listed at 71 hp / 120 ft-lb
sourcing_strategy: Use as lightweight low-voltage mule candidate, not full reference truck
key_questions: What reduced vehicle mass, speed, grade, and payload assumptions would make AC-50/AC-51 acceptable?
notes: Useful for very low-speed/simple prototype work; likely below full utility-truck target.

reference_spec_or_target: Series-wound brushed DC; listed 32 hp, 70 ft-lb, 5500 rpm, 165 lb
sourcing_strategy: Keep as DC-motor comparison/prototype-only candidate
key_questions: Is brushed DC ever acceptable for a repo branch, and what safety/maintenance caveats must be documented?
notes: Real and purchasable, but not up to the main reference architecture: brushes, high current, lower voltage, limited regen/control polish.

reference_spec_or_target: 72-120V series DC motor for small/medium cars; reseller claims up to 62 hp peak with 120V/550A controller
sourcing_strategy: Keep as budget brushed-DC comparison only
key_questions: Can it meet even a reduced kei-truck payload/speed requirement without excessive heat/current?
notes: Better understood as a low-speed/low-cost DC baseline than a serious full-truck candidate.
